Fix npm global install permission error

Configure npm to use user-writable ~/.npm-global directory instead of
system /boot/system/lib/node_modules which requires root privileges.

commit.message:
Fix npm detection: use exit code instead of output string

The previous logic checked if "npm" appeared in output, but error messages
like "which: npm: Command not found" also contain "npm", causing false
positives. Now uses the exit code from `which` directly.

commit.message:
Fix installer to check npm prerequisite first

Reorder installation flow to verify npm (prerequisite) before checking
for Claude Code. This ensures we install dependencies in the correct order.

New flow: check npm → install npm if needed → check claude → install claude if needed

commit.message:
fix(model-3d): preserve heFaceIds in cloneFaceData for group push-pull

cloneFaceData was copying heFaceId (singular) but dropping heFaceIds
(the group array), so extrudeFaceGroup was never called during push-pull.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(model-3d): add face groups for unified outer frame selection and push-pull

After polygon split, outer strip faces are now registered as a face group.
Clicking any strip highlights the entire frame; push-pulling extrudes all
strips together. Adds extrudeFaceGroup() for coordinated group extrusion
with proper wall creation only on the group boundary.

Fix malformed redirect URL in push.php approval workflow

produced an invalid URL like dashboard.php&approval_submitted=1.
Use ? or & depending on whether params already exist.

cron, upload, etc.) automatically retry on stale sessions — not just
dynamic tool execution.

Résultat: le Super Admin Bassi tombait dans le fallback "Voir le profil" sans média. Backend: - /partners/active: lit heroVideos[] du concept, extrait la première URL - Passe le tableau complet heroVideos dans la réponse partenaire - Fallback sur heroVideoUrl/heroImageUrl (champs legacy) si heroVideos vide Frontend PartnersCarousel: - Utilise heroVideos[0].url en priorité, puis video_url, puis heroImageUrl - getMediaInfo détecte images (/api/files/*/image_*) + vidéos (/api/files/*/video_*) - Nouveau rendu pour les images uploadées (avant: seulement

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: extend collection mutable range when for-of loop elements are mutated

Track which named variables are elements of for-of loop collections via
IteratorNext → StoreLocal chains. When a PropertyStore or ComputedStore
mutates such a variable, extend the collection's mutable range to include
that mutation site. This ensures the collection stays inside the same
reactive scope as its element mutations.

Fixes for-of-mutate-item-of-local-collection.js (+1 correct).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(alias): add typo suggestions for unknown step commands (#1363)

When the user types an unknown step command or alias name, suggest the
closest match using Jaro similarity (same algorithm and 0.7 threshold as
clap). Checks both built-in step commands and configured aliases.

Also fixes error message styling per output guidelines: bold for names
instead of quotes, no second-person pronouns ("perhaps" instead of "did
you mean").

Examples:
- `wt step comit` → `Unknown step command comit — perhaps commit?`
- `wt step deplyo` (with alias `deploy`) → `Unknown step command deplyo
— perhaps deploy?`
- `wt step zzz` (no close match) → `Unknown alias zzz (available:
deploy, hello)`

> _This was written by Claude Code on behalf of max-sixty_

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix embed URL stripping on Android by unwrapping spans before matching

Android Chrome may split URLs across multiple text nodes via autocorrect/
composition <span> elements, breaking all text-node-level matching.
Unwrap all spans in the DOM clone first so the URL lands in a single
text node, then fall back to innerHTML regex as last resort.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add GitHub Releases automation and self-update command

This commit implements Option B for CLI distribution: GitHub Releases with
pre-built binaries and automatic updates.

Changes:
- Add goreleaser configuration for cross-platform builds
  - Supports Linux, macOS (Intel/ARM), Windows
  - Creates Homebrew formula and Scoop manifest automatically
  - Generates checksums and release notes

- Add GitHub Actions workflow for automated releases
  - Triggers on version tags (v*.*.*)
  - Uses goreleaser to build and publish binaries
  - Supports GPG signing (optional)

- Add self-update command to CLI
  - Check for updates: agent-todo update --check
  - Update to latest: agent-todo update
  - Auto-detects platform and downloads correct binary
  - Self-replaces executable on success

- Add version information to CLI
  - Version, commit, date, built-by embedded via ldflags
  - --version flag shows detailed build info
  - Config stores version info for update checks

- Update CLI README with new installation methods
  - Homebrew (recommended for macOS/Linux)
  - Scoop (for Windows)
  - Pre-built binaries from GitHub Releases
  - Go install (for developers)
  - Self-update command documentation
  - Agent task commands documentation

To create a release:
  1. Tag a commit: git tag v1.0.0
  2. Push tag: git push origin v1.0.0
  3. GitHub Actions will build and publish binaries automatically

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
